STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 ,J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Alben Yang 
City Attorney· s Office 
250 Hamilton A\·cnuc. 8th Floor 
Palo Alto, CA 9-B0I 
Re: 
Your Requc.,t for Ad,·ice 
Our l•ile No. A-16-140 
Dear Mr. Yang: 
July 18. 2016 
ThiŲ letlcr rc,pomh to ) our rcquc,t for ad\'icc on hchal f of Commi..,sioncr., Eric Rn.,cnhlum 
and Kate Dm, ning of the City of Palo Alto·s Planning and Tran,portalion Commii;sion regarding 
the conflict of intcre..,t provi.,iom, of the Political Reform Act ( the .. Act"· ).1 Plc.isc note that we arc 
only providing advice under the conflict of intcrc.,t prO\ i.,ions of the Act and not under other 
general conflict of interest prohibitions ... uch a., common law conllict of intcrc!-.t or Section I090. 
i\lorco\'er, thi., letter i, ha,cd on the foci.. pre ... entcd. The Fair Political Practices Commi!-.i;ion Joe., 
not act a-; a finder of fact when it renders advice. ( In re Oglesh_,. ( 1975) 1 FPPC Op!-.. 71.) 
QUESTIONS 
I. ls; Commissioner Ro.,cnhlum prohihitcd from taking part in a deci.,ion of whether to 
recommend to the city council change., to the City's inning that would di,farnr commen:ial 
dc\'clopmcnt where his employer. Palantir Tcdrnologic!-.. Inc. ( .. Palantir .. ). lca!->cs and occupic1-
multiplc commercial huilding., in the area'! 
2. i... Cc.rn1mis!-.ioncr Dem ning prohihitccl from taking part in the dcci,ion where her !->pouse i!-1 
employed by Palantir'! 
CONCLUSIONS

on their interests.

rl!'gul..11or) rl!li:n.:m:e, arc to T11lc 2. Dh l',ion 6 l'f th..: California Code of Rcgulat10ns. uni..:,, othern i,c.: md1catc.:d. 


FACTS 
File No. A-16-140 
Page No. 2 
You are the City Attorney for the City of Palo Alla and are requesting advice on behalf of 
Commissioners Eric Rosenblum and Kate Downing of the City of Palo Alla's Planning and 
Transportation Commission ("Commission'"). 
The Palo Alto City Council is considering zoning changes that would di²favor rnmmercial 
development in the University Avenue downt0\\1n area. The Commission will be making 
recommendations to the city council regarding these changes. These changes \vould have an impact 
on the availability and rental rates of commercial office space in downtown Palo Alto. 
Commissioner Rosenblum is employed by Palantir. Commissioner Downing's spouse is 
employed by Palantir. Palanlir is a privately held multinational technology company valued at $20 
billion that is headquartered in downtown Palo Alto where it occupies multiple office buildings. 
The company has offices throughout North America, Europe, the Middle East, A!-lia, 
Australia and New Zealand. "The company controls about 250,000 square feel of office space, or 
10 to 15 percent of the commercial inventory [ in downtown Palo Alto). spread over 23 or so 
huildings. Some of it, lcasc'i arc for a decade or longer. Annual revenue topped S 1.5 billion in 2015, 
sources say, meaning Palantir is bigger than top publicly traded cloud software companies like 
Workday and Service Now. It has 1,800 employees and is growing headcount 30 percent annually 
said the sources ... ":? Palantir's business is unrelated to real estate. You would like to know whether 
Commissioners Rosenblum and Downing may participate in the decisions related to the zoning 
changes near Palantir's headquarter, in downtown Palo Alto. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to inOuence a go\'ernmental decision in which the official has a financial intere,;t. 
(Section 87103.) Section 87103 defines interests from which a conflict of interest may arise. They 
include the following: 
• Any source of income. except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular courǅe of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating S500 or more in value provided or promised to, recei\·ed by, the public oflicial 
within 12 month, prior to the time when the decision is made. 
• Any busincs³ entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. 
Palantir is an intereǆt of Commissioner Rosenblum as his employer and source of income. 
Commissioner Downing has a financial interest in Palantir as a source of income due to her 
spouse·² employment with the company. ("Income" includes any community property intere³t in 
the income of a spouse (Section 82030).) 
P huv_;!h:nhc.:.c.:11111/20 I ti/0 l/12/Lhc-c.:ia-ha1.·l.:cu-!>t:1rt-11p-tha1s-1al.:im?-11, cr-palo-alto.h1ml. 


File No. A-16-J .m 
Page No. 3 
Foreseeahiliry and Materiality: The standard for fore,;;eeability differs depending on 
whether an intere,;;t is explicitly im olved in the decision. Regulation I 870 I pro,·ides: '·A financial 
effect is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable if the financial interest is a named party in, or the 
subject of, a governmental decision before the official or the official's agency:· None of the 
Commissioners' financial interests are explicitly invol\'ed in the decision to recommend the zoning 
changes because Palantir is not the subject of or a named party in the go\'ernmental decisions. Thus. 
pursuant to Regulation 1870 I (b ). if a financial effect can be recognized a<, a realistic possibility and 
more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable. A financial effect need not be 
likely to be considered reasonably foreseeable, howc\'er, if the financial result cannot be expected 
absent extraordinary circum,;tances not subject to the public ofticial"s control. it is not reasonably 
foreseeable. 
Materiality will be found under Regulation 18702.3(a)(-l) if: 
• The source is a business entity and a prudent person with sufficient information would find 
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision· s financial effect would contribute to a change 
in the ,·alue of a pri\'ately-held business entit). ( Regulation 18702.1 (h ).) 
You stule that Palantir is a ··multi-bill ion company"· and has orlices throughout North America. 
Europe. the Middle East. Asia. Australia and New Zealand. You cite a Forbes article stating that 
Palantir was recently , alued at S20 hi Ilion. According to a source quoted in the CNBC article, the 
company has 1.800 employees and its 2015 rc\'cnues topped S 1.5 billion. The sheer size of 
Palantir's business a<, well as the breadth of its world\\ ide presence suggest that a prudent person 
would not find it reasonably foreseeable that the financial effect of a decision affecting the value of 
commercial property leases in downtown Palo Alto would contribute to a change in the \'alue of 
Palantir's business. Under these facts, it i., not reasonably foreseeable that the zoning decisions you 
ha\'e described will have a material financial effect on Commissioners Rosenblum's and Downing·Ǉ 
interests. 
VJ:jgl 
If you ha\'e other questionǈ on this matter. please contact me at (9 I 6) 3 22-5660. 
Sincerely. 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
By: 
Valentina Joyce 
Counsel. Legal Di,·ision
